UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user of the AuraChat application, which can be either a registered user or a temporary guest.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

displayName.description: A user-friendly name chosen by or assigned to the user profile.

isGuest.description: Indicates whether this user profile is for a temporary guest session (true) or a persistent user (false).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

lastActiveAt.description: Timestamp of the last known activity for this user profile.

ChatSession.title: ChatSession
ChatSession.description: Represents a single, continuous conversation thread between a user and the AI.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChatSession entity.

title.description: A user-editable title or a generated summary for the chat session, making it easier to identify in history.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the chat session was first initiated.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the chat session was last updated, typically when a new message was added.

ChatMessage.title: ChatMessage
ChatMessage.description: Represents an individual message within a ChatSession, sent by either the user or the AI.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChatMessage entity.

senderType.description: Indicates who sent the message, e.g., 'user' or 'ai'.

content.description: The textual content of the message.

timestamp.description: The exact time the message was sent or received within the chat session, used for ordering.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles, including details for both registered and guest users. Each document ID (`userId`) corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID. Access is restricted to the owning user. Includes 'isGuest' to identify temporary profiles.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID of the user.

definition.description: Stores individual chat sessions (conversations) for a specific user. Each document ID (`chatSessionId`) identifies a unique conversation thread. Access is restricted to the owning user, whose ID is denormalized in the path for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID of the owner of this chat session.

params.name: chatSessionId
params.description: The unique ID of the chat session.

definition.description: Stores individual messages within a specific chat session. Each document ID (`messageId`) identifies a single message. Access is restricted to the owning user of the parent chat session, with user and session IDs denormalized in the path for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID of the owner of the parent chat session.

params.name: chatSessionId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent chat session.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique ID of the chat message.

firestore.reasoning:
The proposed Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence and secure `list` operations (QAPs) by leveraging hierarchical, path-based ownership. 

1.  **Authorization Independence (Denormalization):** Access control is primarily achieved by embedding the `userId` directly into the path for all user-specific data. For example, a user's profile is at `/users/{userId}`, their chat sessions are at `/users/{userId}/chatSessions/{chatSessionId}`, and messages within a session are at `/users/{userId}/chatSessions/{chatSessionId}/chatMessages/{messageId}`. This means that Firestore security rules can directly compare `request.auth.uid` with the `{userId}` wildcard in the path, without needing to perform `get()` operations on parent documents. This prevents cascading authorization dependencies, simplifies rule logic, and allows for atomic operations (e.g., creating a session and its first message in a single batch).

2.  **Structural Segregation & QAPs:** Each collection (`users`, `chatSessions`, `chatMessages`) is designed with a homogeneous security posture. All `UserProfile` documents are private to their respective users. Similarly, all `ChatSession` and `ChatMessage` documents within a user's subcollections are private to that user. This segregation ensures that `list` queries are inherently secure.
    *   To list a user's profiles: Only the authenticated user can read their own profile document (`/users/{request.auth.uid}`). A global `list` on `/users` would not be allowed without a specific `{userId}` matching `request.auth.uid`.
    *   To list a user's chat sessions: A user can securely query `/users/{request.auth.uid}/chatSessions`. The security rules will ensure that the `{userId}` in the path matches `request.auth.uid`, thereby automatically filtering results to only include the current user's sessions. 
    *   To list messages in a specific session: A user can securely query `/users/{request.auth.uid}/chatSessions/{chatSessionId}/chatMessages`. Again, the path-based authorization ensures only messages for the requesting user and specified session are accessible. 

3.  **DBAC & Guest Users:** User roles (though only implicit ownership here) are stored in the database (implicitly via the `userId` in the path and `isGuest` in `UserProfile`). Both registered and guest users (via Firebase Anonymous Auth) are treated uniformly at the database level, with their data residing under their respective `request.auth.uid` (which will be an anonymous UID for guests). The `isGuest` field provides additional context within the `UserProfile` document itself.

This design provides a clear, secure, and scalable foundation for AuraChat, making security rules straightforward and highly performant.
